input.query: Is it true that EVMs can be hacked via Bluetooth?

output.explanation: EVMs used in Indian elections are standalone machines with no network connectivity. They do not have Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, or any wireless communication hardware. The ECI has repeatedly demonstrated that these machines are mathematically and physically tamper-proof in their current configuration.

promises.promise: Mahalakshmi Scheme: ₹1 lakh per year to one woman from every poor family.
promises.context: Part of the 'Nyay' pillars focusing on direct cash transfers.

promises.promise: Pehli Naukri Pakki: Right to Apprenticeship for every diploma/degree holder.
promises.context: Ensuring one year of formal employment training with a stipend.

steps.title: Identification Challenge
steps.content: A polling agent can challenge your identity by depositing a small fee (₹2) with the Presiding Officer.

steps.title: Summary Inquiry
steps.content: The Presiding Officer conducts a summary inquiry, checking your identity against the electoral roll and documents.

steps.title: Verdict
steps.content: If the challenge is not established, you are allowed to vote. If identity is found to be false, you may be handed over to the police.
